_comment: Aran Technologies — LiDAR Stress Test Scenarios. Each scenario has a name, description, and a list of obstacle events. Each event fires at t=start_s seconds after scenario begins, injects the obstacle for duration_s seconds, at dist_m metres and bearing_deg degrees (drone-relative). Multiple events can overlap to simulate simultaneous obstacles.

scenarios.name: single_frontal
scenarios.description: Baseline — one obstacle dead ahead at 4.5m. Validates basic avoidance trigger.

scenarios.name: rear_obstacle
scenarios.description: Obstacle at 180deg (directly behind). PID should NOT trigger — rear is not a threat. Validates bearing filtering.

scenarios.name: side_obstacle_left
scenarios.description: Obstacle at 270deg (left flank). Tests lateral avoidance direction.

scenarios.name: side_obstacle_right
scenarios.description: Obstacle at 90deg (right flank). Mirror of left-flank test.

scenarios.name: rapid_approach
scenarios.description: Obstacle starts at warning zone (9m) and ramps in to 2m over 3 seconds. Tests PID response to fast-closing obstacle.

scenarios.name: rapid_withdrawal
scenarios.description: Obstacle appears at 3m then instantly clears. Tests that PID resets cleanly and mission resumes without lingering avoidance.

scenarios.name: dual_simultaneous
scenarios.description: Two obstacles at the same time — one at 45deg (4m), one at 315deg (5m). Tests nearest-obstacle priority logic.

scenarios.name: triple_simultaneous
scenarios.description: Three simultaneous obstacles — front, left, right. Maximum stress on nearest-pick logic. Only nearest should drive avoidance.

scenarios.name: sequential_gauntlet
scenarios.description: Four obstacles in sequence from different bearings with short gaps. Tests repeated avoidance + resume cycles.

scenarios.name: oscillating_distance
scenarios.description: Obstacle alternates between 4m (avoidance) and 8m (warning) every second. Tests PID integral windup and reset stability.

scenarios.name: tight_gains_frontal
scenarios.description: Same as single_frontal but with aggressive PID gains. Tests faster response vs oscillation risk.

scenarios.name: tight_gains_gauntlet
scenarios.description: Sequential gauntlet with aggressive PID gains. Cross-compare response time vs default gains.

scenarios.name: iit_panel_demo
scenarios.description: Clean scripted demo sequence for IIT panel presentation. One clear avoidance event, then clear passage. Deterministic and visually readable on GCS.

scenarios.name: urban_canyon
scenarios.description: Simulates a tight urban corridor — walls at 90deg (right, 4m) and 270deg (left, 4.5m) throughout the run, with a frontal pillar at t=6s. Classic city-block scenario: drone must navigate a narrow lane and dodge a protrusion. Tests whether the escape logic correctly favours the wider gap rather than blindly going left.

scenarios.name: pop_up_ambush
scenarios.description: Obstacle pops up from below (simulated rooftop protrusion or balloon) at zero warning — distance jumps instantly to 2.5m at 0deg. Tests whether the debounce+avoidance pipeline reacts fast enough with tight PID gains when there is no gradual approach phase.

scenarios.name: crossing_fast_mover
scenarios.description: Simulates a fast-moving obstacle (bird, counter-UAV) that sweeps across from 90deg to 0deg in quick succession. Short duration events at 3m create near-miss windows that shift bearing rapidly. Tests bearing-tracking stability of the avoidance loop and escape-side flip logic when the threat changes quadrant mid-event.

scenarios.name: diagonal_corridor
scenarios.description: Two angled walls at 45deg and 225deg, creating a diagonal slot the drone must thread. Then a third obstacle blocks the slot forcing a climb escape. Tests that sector binning in world-frame correctly identifies the only clear channel (N or S) and routes the detour through it.

scenarios.name: five_obstacle_box
scenarios.description: Five simultaneous obstacles forming a near-enclosure: N(3.5m), NE(4.5m), E(4m), W(4m), NW(4.5m). Only S is clear. Tests sector analysis under maximum congestion — the escape logic must correctly identify the only open sector and route the drone South.

scenarios.name: staggered_sweep
scenarios.description: Eight obstacles fire one at a time, stepping clockwise through all 8 sectors (0, 45, 90 ... 315deg). Each lasts 4s with a 2s gap before the next. A full 360deg sweep that exhaustively validates avoidance at every bearing, including the problematic 90/270 (side) and 180 (rear) bearings.

scenarios.name: altitude_stack
scenarios.description: Three sequential obstacles all dead-ahead but at increasing closeness: 9m (warning), 5m (avoid), then 2m (hard avoid). Each clears before the next appears. Mimics approaching a tower face at speed — validates that each avoidance event increments the counter exactly once and that the PID integral fully resets between events.

scenarios.name: persistent_blocker_climb_test
scenarios.description: A single immovable obstacle dead-ahead at 3m that never clears. Designed to trigger the AVOIDANCE_TIMEOUT climb-escape path. The obstacle remains for 20s, well beyond the 10s AVOIDANCE_TIMEOUT_S threshold. Validates the full timeout->climb->clear pipeline and confirms that horizontal detour commands cease once the climb is active.

scenarios.name: multi_target_recon
scenarios.description: ISR-specific: simulates flying past three separate point-of-interest structures, each generating a brief side-obstacle contact as the drone passes close. Obstacles appear at alternating 80deg and 280deg at 5s intervals to mimic building facades during a recon sweep. Tests that the system logs each as a distinct avoidance event rather than one continuous event.

scenarios.name: formation_break
scenarios.description: Two friendly drones in simulated formation: one at 30deg (6m) and one at 330deg (6m). Then a third hostile obstacle enters dead-ahead at 3m while the formation obstacles persist. Tests that nearest-obstacle logic correctly prioritises the 3m frontal threat over the 6m formation members, and that only the frontal event fires avoidance.

scenarios.name: night_isr_low_speed
scenarios.description: Slow, careful night-recon pass at reduced speed (simulated via tight soft gains). Multiple low-confidence obstacles at warning range (18-24m), one of which closes to 6m. Tests that soft PID gains produce smooth, minimal corrections rather than aggressive jerks at low speed — suitable for low-observable night ISR profiles.
